summary: Dependency-confusion beacon on preinstall to attacker-controlled pingback host
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` hook runs `node index.js`, which performs an HTTPS GET to `https://4b84b8ub.pingback.sh/npm-installed`. The package's name shadows a private/internal namespace (`fork-angular-daterangepicker`) and self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept. Installing the package automatically signals the third-party operator of `pingback.sh` that the name resolved against the public registry on a target machine, leaking the installer's source IP and confirming that the private name is exploitable for dependency-confusion follow-ups. No legitimate functionality is delivered.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
